metadata.title: GPS compared to long-term geologic motion of the north arm of Sulawesi
metadata.study_area: North arm of Sulawesi; Celebes Sea; Palu-Koro Fault transect
metadata.method: GPS (GEODYSSEA network campaigns Nov/Dec 1994 + Apr 1996; local PKF transect 1992-1997); palaeomagnetic comparison; magnetic anomaly analysis of Celebes Sea
conclusions: North Arm of Sulawesi has rotated 20-25° clockwise since 5 Ma about a pole NE of Sulawesi (2.1°N, 126.2°E); GPS rotation rate 3.4 ± 0.4°/Ma matches geological estimate of minimum 4°/Ma
PKF GPS slip rate (far-field): 4 cm/yr (3.8-4.0 cm/yr from AMPA vs Kalimantan stations); consistent with geological minimum of 4 cm/yr from 200 km / 5 Ma displacement
PKF local transect (Palu, 5-year avg): 34 mm/yr average; variable 26 mm/yr (1992-1995) to 63 mm/yr (1995-1997) due to seismic transients in northern Sulawesi
Celebes Sea: ~250 km subducted south beneath North Sulawesi Trench since last 5 Ma; south-dipping slab to ~200 km depth blocked at depth in east by north-dipping Molucca Sea slab

Una-Una Island (Gulf of Tomini) = youngest (still active) expression of CAK volcanic chain that migrated NW with rotating North Arm; volcanic chain extends ~200 km wide NW-SE belt from North Sulawesi to Una-Una

properties.name: Palu-Koro Fault
properties.slip_rate_gps_method: GEODYSSEA far-field (AMPA vs Kalimantan stations); 17-month observation, 2 campaigns
properties.slip_rate_gps_transect_note: 5-year average on 50 km transect across fault; variable 26-63 mm/yr (seismic transients affect near-fault stations)
properties.slip_rate_geological_note: Minimum 4 cm/yr from ~200 km displacement in ~5 Ma (Silver et al. 1983 reconstruction)
properties.context: Western limit of the clockwise-rotating Sula/North Arm microblock. PKF accommodates the sinistral component of the oblique collision between Sula platform and North Arm. Consistent with Silver et al. (1983) reconstruction.

properties.name: Euler Pole — North Arm (Sula Block) Clockwise Rotation (GPS)
properties.model: GPS rigid-block fitting (GEODYSSEA + Palu transect; 4 stations inside block)
properties.interpretation: North Arm of Sulawesi (welded to the Sula domain since ~15 Ma) has been rotating clockwise about this pole since ~5 Ma. The rotation drives subduction of Celebes Sea crust at the North Sulawesi Trench and sinistral slip on the PKF. Station MANA (near Manado) is close to the pole — motion similar to Eurasian plate. AMPA (central Sulawesi, east of PKF) moves westward relative to MANA, consistent with this rotation.

properties.name: GPS Station AMPA — Key Velocity Reference (Sula Block)
properties.description: GEODYSSEA GPS campaign station AMPA in central Sulawesi (east side of PKF, Sula domain). Used as the primary observation point for far-field velocity of Sula block relative to Kalimantan (Eurasian plate).
properties.velocity_note: 4.3-4.5 cm/yr oriented 307-318° relative to Kalimantan stations BATU and TAWA. Strike-slip projection on PKF (azimuth 345°) gives 3.8-4.0 cm/yr left-lateral.

properties.name: Una-Una Volcano (Gulf of Tomini) — Youngest CAK Volcanic Chain
properties.description: Una-Una Island volcano in Gulf of Tomini. Youngest (still active as of 1998, last eruption 1983) expression of the calc-alkalic potassic (CAK) volcanic chain that migrated northwestward with the rotating North Arm. The chain extends ~200 km NW-SE from North Sulawesi to Una-Una. The CAK volcanism is interpreted to result from subduction of Sula-Buton continental crust below central-western Sulawesi (Polve et al. 1997). Una-Una is currently closest to the volcanic source, sitting above the active 'melting continental crust' — consistent with the Hall & Spakman (2015) interpretation of Sula slab delamination.
